methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 10 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Signaloid is a Seed-stage semiconductor company that has raised $10M to date. The relevant comp universe spans the Chips & Semiconductors sector, which includes 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers such as Cerebras, SambaNova Systems, and PsiQuantum.

A single valuation method applies here: stage-progression analysis. Using the $10M total raised as a valuation proxy, a typical Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $25M. A peer-median approach is not applicable due to insufficient narrow-filter matches within the same stage and valuation tier—while 10 same-sector peers exist, fewer than 3 meet the adjacent-stage or similar-tier criteria needed for reliable median derivation.

The triangulated valuation range is $21M to $29M, with a median point of $25M. This analysis reflects a single methodology and carries material uncertainty around (i) whether the raised capital adequately proxies enterprise value at this stage, and (ii) the absence of direct peer-comparable data to validate or adjust the stage-progression multiple. Additional data on recent Seed-stage semiconductor exits or later-round valuations in this cohort would sharpen confidence in this range.
